name: neomodel (Neo4j Object Graph Mapper)

performance.notes: OGM abstraction adds overhead; Cypher queries via official neo4j driver

agent_readiness.notes: Neo4j OGM for Python — Django-style model definitions. from neomodel import config, StructuredNode, StringProperty, IntegerProperty, RelationshipTo. config.DATABASE_URL = 'bolt://neo4j:password@localhost:7687'. class Person(StructuredNode): name = StringProperty(unique_index=True). age = IntegerProperty(). friends = RelationshipTo('Person', 'KNOWS'). alice = Person(name='Alice', age=30).save(). bob = Person(name='Bob', age=25).save(). alice.friends.connect(bob). Person.nodes.filter(age__gte=25). person.friends.all(). Relationships with properties: from neomodel import StructuredRel, DateProperty. class KnowsRel(StructuredRel): since = DateProperty(). Neo4jDateProperty, ArrayProperty, JSONProperty. batch: db.install_all_labels(). Django integration: neomodel django extras.

gotchas: neomodel 5.x requires Neo4j 5.x — check compatibility with your Neo4j version
No type annotations — limited IDE support
Complex traversals still require raw Cypher — OGM doesn't cover all graph patterns
config.DATABASE_URL is global state — testing isolation requires care
best_when: Django-style OGM for Neo4j (familiar class-based model definitions)
When Cypher knowledge is limited but graph DB needed
Projects needing clear model definitions with property validation
avoid_when: Complex graph queries requiring full Cypher — use neo4j driver directly
When neo4j official driver with Cypher is preferred for clarity
summary: neomodel is a Neo4j OGM. class Person(StructuredNode): name = StringProperty(); friends = RelationshipTo('Person', 'KNOWS'). Person.nodes.filter(age__gte=25). Relationship properties via StructuredRel. Django-style model definitions. MIT by neo4j-contrib. Best for teams wanting ORM-like Neo4j access. neomodel 5.x requires Neo4j 5.x. No async. Complex queries need raw Cypher. Global config state complicates testing.
